docs.title: Assesment and Advisory
docs.description: What are Cloud Assessment Services?Cloud Assessment and Advisory is a structured engagement that thoroughly evaluates an organization’s IT infrastructure, applications, and processes to determine cloud readiness. The outcome is a tailored roadmap for migration and modernization aligned with business objectives, operational needs, and future scalability.

docs.title: Cloud Assure
docs.description: What is Cloud Assure Service (CAS)?Cloud Assure is a comprehensive cloud management service suite designed to help enterprises manage, secure, and optimize their cloud environments across public (Yntraa Cloud, AWS, Azure), private, hybrid, and multi-cloud setups. It covers the full lifecycle from assessment and migration to operations, security, optimization, and professional services.

docs.title: Migration Assist
docs.description: What is Cloud Migration Assist?Cloud Migration Assist is a structured service that supports all types of migrations, including on-premises to cloud, cloud-to-cloud, and legacy environment migrations. It ensures secure and efficient movement of workloads, applications, databases, and data with minimal disruption and a controlled transition.

docs.title: Professional Service
docs.description: What is Cloud Professional Service?Cloud Professional Service is a specialized, project-based consulting and implementation offering under the Cloud Assure portfolio. It helps organizations maximize cloud adoption, transformation, and innovation through tailored engagements spanning the entire cloud lifecycle—from strategy and adoption to optimization and continuous improvement.

docs.title: Managed Databases
docs.description: What is Yntraa Cloud Managed Database as a Service (MDBaaS)?Yntraa Cloud MDBaaS is a fully managed database solution that offers enterprises scalable, secure, and high-performance databases without the need to manage the underlying infrastructure.

docs.title: Resiliency Assurance Service
docs.description: What is a Disaster?Disaster is a natural or man-made (or technological) hazard resulting in an event of substantial extent causing significant physical damage or destruction or loss.

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
docs.description: What happens if I miss a payment due date?Depending on how your service provider has set up the payment collections policies, one of the following might happen:

docs.title: File Storage
docs.description: What is File Storage in STaaS?File Storage offers shared access using standard file protocols (NFS/SMB), suitable for collaborative workspaces, media editing, and centralized storage environments.

docs.title: What is IPv4 Networking?
docs.description: Internet Protocol version 4(IPv4) is the fourth generation of the Internet Protocol, and it remains the most commonly used system for assigning addresses to devices connected to a network. An IPv4 address is made up of four numbers separated by dots—like 10.0.1.15—and each address is unique, ensuring that data sent over the internet reaches the right device.

next.title: What is a Virtual Firewall in the Cloud?

docs.title: What is a Virtual Firewall in the Cloud?
docs.description: A virtual firewall is a software-based tool used to protect cloud systems. Just like a physical firewall protects a building from fire, a virtual firewall helps protect your cloud network from harmful or unwanted traffic. It controls the flow of data between different parts of your system and blocks anything suspicious or risky. This keeps your applications and data safe, whether traffic is coming from outside the cloud or moving between parts of the same system.

docs.title: What is a VPC?
docs.description: A Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) is a secure, isolated section of a public cloud where you can deploy and manage your cloud resources—such as virtual machines, databases, and applications—with full control over networking and security. With a VPC, you define your own IP address ranges, create subnets, configure routing tables, and set up firewalls and gateways, allowing you to build a custom network architecture tailored to your needs.

previous.title: What is a Virtual Firewall in the Cloud?

docs.title: What is Instances?
docs.description: Cloud computing allows users to run virtual machines called instances, which are flexible, on-demand servers that can run different operating systems. The two most common types of instances available in cloud environments are Linux instances and Windows instances. Both serve unique purposes and cater to different application needs.

next.title: What is IP Address, Subnet, Tier and CIDR?

docs.title: What is IP Address, Subnet, Tier and CIDR?
docs.description: Every device or service in a network—whether it's a virtual machine, load balancer, or database—needs a unique IP address to talk to other devices. It’s like a digital address for communication. For example, a VM may be assigned 192.168.1.10, which allows it to send and receive data across the network.

docs.title: Version 1.0.0.0
docs.description: We are excited to announce the general availability of Yntraa Cloud Platform, a modern cloud infrastructure platform designed to support enterprise applications, data platforms, and digital services.

docs.title: Subscriptions and Services
docs.description: A subscription is a record created whenever a new resource is purchased or a service is activated. In other words, a subscription is a financial ‘agreement’ that links the item that has been purchased, its value, and its renewal frequency.

docs.title: Assessment and Advisory
docs.description: Cloud Assessment and Advisory Service helps organizations assess their current IT landscape and define a clear, strategic roadmap for cloud adoption. It provides expert guidance and tailored recommendations to ensure a secure, cost-effective, and well-aligned cloud transformation.

docs.title: Migration Assist
docs.description: Cloud Migration Service enables the secure and seamless transition of applications, data, and infrastructure to cloud or hybrid environments. Through a structured approach and tailored migration strategies, it minimizes risk and disruption while accelerating the benefits of cloud adoption.

docs.title: Monitoring and Notifications
docs.description: Cloud Monitoring and Notification Service ensures continuous visibility into the health and performance of cloud resources through real-time monitoring, centralized logging, and automated alerts. It enables timely issue detection and response, helping maintain uptime,

docs.title: Professional Services
docs.description: Cloud Professional Services provide structured, expert-led support to help organizations plan, implement, and optimize their cloud environments. These services cover the complete cloud lifecycle, including strategy, migration, modernization, and ongoing improvement.

docs.title: About Kubernetes
docs.description: Yntraa Cloud users the ability to deploy highly-available Kubernetes clusters for their cloud-native development, deployment and DevOps use cases. The Yntraa Kubernetes Service extends basic and advanced cluster management functionalities via the cloud console UI and the kubectl utility.

docs.description: The Kubernetes Dashboard is a web-based user interface that provides a visual representation of a cluster's resources and enables easier management and monitoring. Here's an overview of the dashboard:

docs.title: Accessing a Cluster using the Command Line
docs.description: Kubernetes clusters can be accessed and controlled from the command line using the kubeconfig manifest for the cluster. Each cluster has a unique manifest which is required to identify and target the cluster using the kubectl utility.

docs.title: About RHEL Instances
docs.description: Instances are virtualized compute resources designed to support the execution of applications and workloads. Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) instances are virtual machines provisioned with RHEL operating system images, enabling a stable and enterprise-grade environment for deployment and operations.

docs.description: Alerts get triggered whenever a configured condition is met. You can create multiple alerts on an instance. Alerts are sent to recipients that you can define and manage.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.description: After completing the sign-up process, you receive an email with the details to access the cloud console.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.description: Yntraa Cloud is a powerful, all-in-one solution designed to make managing your cloud and network services simple and efficient. Whether you are a business, an organization, or an individual, Yntraa Cloud provides everything you need to keep your digital infrastructure running smoothly.

docs.title: DDoS Protection
docs.description: Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attack is a cyberattack that disrupts a website, server, or network by overwhelming it with excessive traffic from multiple compromised systems. This makes services unavailable to legitimate users and can severely impact business operations.

docs.title: Privileged Access Management
docs.description: In modern IT environments, Privileged Access Management (PAM) protects critical systems and sensitive data by securely managing and controlling privileged accounts. While PAM safeguards privileged credentials and enforces strict access controls, it also monitors privileged sessions and activities to prevent misuse or unauthorized access, ensuring improved security, visibility, and control across on-premises and cloud environments.

docs.title: Yotta Smart CSOC
docs.description: Yotta Smart Complementary Subservice Organization Controls (CSOC) is a security operations service designed to help organizations effectively manage and respond to growing and complex cyber threats. It combines advanced monitoring and automated response capabilities with expert support to detect, investigate, and remediate security incidents quickly.

docs.title: Backup and Recovery
docs.description: Backup and Recovery is a managed service that protects critical business data by creating secure backups and enabling fast restoration. It provides resilience against data loss, cyber threats, and system failures by maintaining reliable recovery points and reducing downtime.

docs.title: Bare Metal
docs.description: Bare Metal Service provides direct access to dedicated physical servers without virtualization overhead, ensuring optimal performance, low latency, and predictable throughput for performance-critical workloads.

docs.title: DevOps as a Service
docs.description: To activate the desired DevOps service, perform the following steps:

docs.title: Load Testing as a Service
docs.description: In cloud services, Load Testing as a Service (LTaaS) helps organizations evaluate the performance, scalability, and stability of applications by simulating real-world user traffic and workloads. It enables teams to identify performance bottlenecks, measure system behavior under different load conditions, and ensure that applications can handle peak demand efficiently, thereby improving reliability, user experience, and overall application performance in cloud environments.

docs.title: Managed Database Services
docs.description: Managed Databases is a cloud-based service that takes care of database setup, maintenance, scaling, and security, so organizations do not have to manage it themselves. It supports both relational and non-relational databases, ensuring high performance, availability, and reliability.

docs.title: Messaging Services
docs.description: In cloud services, Messaging Service enables organisations to build event-driven and real-time applications using managed messaging platforms like RabbitMQ and Kafka. It provides reliable queues and event streams without the complexity of managing infrastructure, ensuring high availability, security, and performance while teams focus on application development.

docs.title: Resiliency Assurance Service
docs.description: Resiliency Assurance Service is a disaster recovery, focused solution designed to help organizations quickly restore critical infrastructure, applications, and data after disruptions.

docs.title: About L2 Networks
docs.description: L2 networks, often called data link layer networks, play a crucial role in computer networking by establishing direct connections between devices in a local network and ensuring data packets are delivered accurately to their intended destination.

docs.title: Adding New L2 Network
docs.description: To add a new L2 network.

docs.title: About Load Balancer Instances
docs.description: The Yntraa Load Balancer Instances (LBI) Service allows you to create load balancer appliances, which are Linux Instances pre-loaded with a Citrix NetScaler VPX image. LBI can be used with VPC networks to achieve various traffic management features that are not possible using the standard VPC-based load balancing.

docs.title: Activating the NetScaler VPX Control Panel
docs.description: Adding the NetScaler VPX is required to enable advanced load balancing, traffic management, and security features for your application environment.

docs.title: Creating a Load Balancer Instance
docs.description: Load Balancer Instances can be created inside a VPC by following these simple steps:

next.title: Activating the NetScaler VPX Control Panel

docs.title: About NAT Gateways
docs.description: A Network Address Translation (NAT) gateway is a networking component in cloud and on-premises environments that enables resources within a private subnet (such as virtual machines or containers) to connect to the internet or other external networks, without exposing these resources directly to incoming connections from those external networks. This helps in enhancing security in cloud infrastructure by keeping resources in private subnets isolated from direct public access.

docs.title: Instances
docs.description: This section lists all the instances that are a part of this Virtual Firewall.

docs.title: IPv4 Addresses
docs.description: Each virtual gateway comes with a default IPv4 address that belongs to the Virtual Gateway governing the network. The default (source - NAT) IPv4 address can be used to access the Virtual Gateway publicly or via Remote Access (RA) VPN.

docs.title: Operations
docs.description: You can perform the following operations in the Operations section:

next.title: Port Forwarding for VM via VNF

docs.title: Overview
docs.description: Navigate to Nat Gateways tab and access the Overview tab to view the following details:

docs.title: Viewing and Managing NAT Gateways
docs.description: To manage a NAT gateway, follow these steps:

docs.title: Port Forwarding for VM via VNF
docs.description: This section outlines the steps to configure port forwarding for a Virtual Machine (VM) using a Virtual Network Function (VNF) in a cloud environment. Port forwarding allows external clients to securely access services (for example SSH, HTTP) hosted on the VM by mapping ports from the VNF’s public IP to the VM’s private IP. This approach improves network segmentation and control by routing all incoming traffic through the VNF layer.

docs.title: Port Forwarding with Public IP
docs.description: In Yntraa Cloud, an additional Public IP enables you to expose internal services hosted on virtual machines (VMs) to the internet by creating dedicated port forwarding rules. This approach is especially useful when multiple external IPs are needed for services such as SSH, HTTP, or custom applications. By assigning specific public IPs to particular services or VMs, you can achieve greater control, flexibility, and network segmentation.

previous.title: Port Forwarding for VM via VNF

docs.description: The Virtual Firewall Service is delivered as an integration using OS images pre-loaded with Yntraa Software Firewall.

docs.title: Adding Instances to a VFI-based Network
docs.description: Instances can be created and placed in the L2-LAN (Free Networks) created by the Virtual Firewall. These Instances use special Linux OS images, which are available as a separate OS Collection. You can create Linux Instances, and choose the appropriate options/controls to create an Instance in the Virtual Firewall network.

This setup allows you to route VM traffic through the VNF, enabling secure and segmented network environments.

docs.description: Firewall Instances provide the ability to implement firewall services using a Virtualized Network Function (VNF) framework within your network environment. These instances are designed to support multiple VLAN configurations, enable the use of public IPv4 addresses, and offer automated service deployment.

docs.title: Create, List and View VPCs
docs.description: Creating a VPC

docs.title: Creating VPC Subnets and Tiers
docs.description: VPCs follow the convention of 3-tiered architectures, with web, app, and DB tiers forming the norm. You can, however, configure these tiers to suit your application architecture or just follow the common convention.

docs.title: IPv4 Addresses and VPC
docs.description: IPv4 Addresses are an integral part of using VPC networking, and need to be used to access various components of the VPC. By default, a public IPv4 Address is assigned to the VR which can communicate through the internet to transmit traffic to/from the VR. This IPv4 can also be used for configuring remote access (L2TP) and site-to-site (IPSec) VPN connections.

next.title: Managing Access Control on VPC Subnets

docs.title: Managing Access Control on VPC Subnets
docs.description: Access control policies can be created using Access Control Lists (ACL) and configuring rules within these ACL (called ACL Rules). An ACL can then be applied to any tier within the VPC. These policies govern what traffic is allowed or restricted into and from your VPC network tiers.

docs.title: Containers
docs.description: To activate the desired Container Registry service, perform the following steps:

docs.title: File Storage
docs.description: File Storage organizes data in a hierarchical structure similar to traditional network drives, enabling easy file sharing and collaboration. It is ideal for document storage, shared workspaces, and everyday file access across users and applications.

docs.title: Signing In
docs.description: To Sign In to your Yntraa Cloud, follow these steps:

docs.title: Signing Up
docs.description: To get started with the Yntraa Cloud, you must create an account on the Yntraa Cloud.

docs.title: About Block Volumes
docs.description: Yntraa block volumes are the underlying software-defined volume management layer that powers various storage-related or storage-driven cloud services. Block Volumes can be used with Linux and Windows instances as root and data disks, with Kubernetes as persistent storage, or simply as a storehouse for snapshots and images.

docs.title: Creating Data Disk
docs.description: To create a Block Volume data disk, follow the below steps:

docs.title: Operations
docs.description: To view all available Block Volume operations, navigate to the Storage, select the Block Volumes and access the Operations tab. The following window appears:

docs.title: Overview
docs.description: Navigate to the Overview tab to view the following details:

docs.title: Resize the Block Volume
docs.description: To resize an available Block Volumes, navigate to Storage, select the Block Volumes and access the Resize tab.

docs.title: Creating Automated Restore Point Schedules
docs.description: Yntraa Cloud users can create restore point policies for their root and data disks. These schedules enable for strategic management of data retention policies and can be used as an alternative backup solution. To create or manage the restore point schedules, navigate to the Schedules section of disk details.

docs.title: Working with Disk Restore Points
docs.description: Yntraa block volumes service provides extensive functionality for managing disk restore points. Restore points are point-in-time 'images' of a disk contents and can be used as a restoration point for the parent disk. The following sections outline all available restore points functions and capabilities in the Yntraa Cloud.

docs.title: Using Block Volumes with Kubernetes
docs.description: Yntraa Kubernetes also uses the underlying Block Volumes service to create persistent volumes for Kubernetes clusters. Yntraa Cloud users can specify the root disk size for each cluster node (control and worker), and the Block Volume service creates the nodes in the cluster with a root disk of the specified size.

docs.title: Working with Instance Volumes
docs.description: Each Instance on Yntraa Cloud (Linux or Windows) includes a 'root' disk or volume. This is the primary disk partition on the Instance, where the operating system, data, components and files reside on the root disk.

docs.title: About Yntraa Cloud Object Storage
docs.description: Yntraa cloud object storage provides scalable and flexible storage for unstructured data in the form of objects. In object storage, data is stored as discrete objects containing data, metadata, and a unique identifier. Yntraa Cloud delivers the service through an integration with Object Storage Platform.

docs.title: Feature List and Integration Details
docs.description: Yntraa Storage is delivered as an integration with a leading object storage platform. The integration functions using API-driven checkpoints.

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
docs.description: What is object storage in the context of Yntraa Cloud?object storage is a scalable and durable storage solution for managing unstructured data such as files, images, videos, and backups.

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.description: To ensure optimal performance and full functionality of the Yntraa Cloud, users must meet the following system requirements:

docs.title: About Tools and Utilities
docs.description: The Tools and Utilities sections in the Yntraa Cloud keep a record of cloud management utilities and assets that might be needed to use specific sections of the Yntraa Cloud.

next.title: Managing SSH Keys and Key Pairs for Linux Instances

docs.title: Adding VPN Gateways
docs.description: VPN Gateways allow you to establish secure connections between networks and cloud resources. The Yntraa Cloud Console enables you to create and manage VPN Gateways to ensure safe and reliable communication using encrypted tunnels over the internet.

previous.title: Managing Instance and Disk Restore Points

docs.title: Managing Custom Templates and Images
docs.description: Custom OS templates can be created from a Restore Point. To view custom templates, navigate to Tools and Utilities > My Images. The following details are displayed:

previous.title: Managing SSH Keys and Key Pairs for Linux Instances

next.title: Managing Instance and Disk Restore Points

docs.title: Managing Instance and Disk Restore Points
docs.description: Restore points use the Yntraa Block Volumes service and occupy billable storage space.

docs.title: Managing SSH Keys and Key Pairs for Linux Instances
docs.description: The Yntraa Cloud allows you to create secure shell connections to Instances in your account by using SSH keys, in addition to using Instance passwords. Since passwords can be easily compromised, SSH keys provide a more secure alternative. These keys function as encrypted signatures and work only when their public and private components match.

link.description: A Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) is a software-defined networking capability on Yntraa Cloud that allows cloud users to simulate a traditional private cloud environment on a public cloud. Using VPC, users can control a host of features and configurations including subnet/tier management, access control using ACL, NAT -ing using IPv4, managing site-to-site or remote VPN connections, or in some advanced cases, terminating their MPLS connections directly on the VPC. In Yntraa, VPC is delivered using a virtual router (VR).
A Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) is a software-defined networking capability on Yntraa Cloud that allows cloud users to simulate a traditional private cloud environment on a public cloud.
A Network Address Translation (NAT) gateway is a networking component in cloud and on-premises environments that enables resources within a private subnet (such as virtual machines or containers).
A Network Address Translation (NAT) gateway is a networking component in cloud and on-premises environments that enables resources within a private subnet (such as virtual machines or containers).

L2 networks, often called data link layer networks, play a crucial role in computer networking by establishing direct connections between devices in a local network and ensuring data packets are delivered accurately to their intended destination.

Virtual Firewalls enables you to use Virtual Firewalls in a Virtualized Network Function (VNF) style, which include support for multiple VLANs and Public IPv4 addresses and automated service activation. This allows for more robust firewall management capabilities.
Yntraa Cloud supports the delivery and management of Virtual Firewall Instances (VFI) in an as-a-service model.
The Yntraa Load Balancer Instances (LBI) Service allows cloud console users to create load balancer appliances, which are Linux Instances pre-loaded with a Citrix NetScaler VPX image.

Enable seamless connectivity between cloud infrastructure and resources, providing efficient data flow and communication. It manages virtual networks, firewalls, and security groups to ensure secure and scalable cloud operations.
Instances are computing units that provide you with resources to run your applications/workloads. Windows Instances are virtual machines that run the images of Windows Operating Systems.
Instances are computing units that provide you with resources to run your applications/workloads. RHEL Instances are virtual machines that run the images of RHEL Operating System.

Before creating a Linux instance, it is important to plan the architecture, networking and access to the Linux Instances. Yntraa cloud platform offers a highly usable and visual way of working with and operating Linux Instances using the Yntraa cloud platform.

Yntraa offers cloud platform users the ability to deploy highly available Kubernetes clusters for their cloud-native development, deployment and DevOps use cases. The Yntraa Kubernetes Service extends basic and advanced cluster management functionalities via the Cloud platform UI and the kubectl utility.

Instances are computing units that provide you with resources to run your applications/workloads. Linux Instances are virtual machines that run the images of Linux Operating Systems, for example, Ubuntu, CentOS, Rocky, Oracle, Debian, etc.
Service providers, on Yntraa cloud, get access to the following collection of interfaces and control panels to operate various aspects of their cloud operations.
Service providers, on Yntraa cloud, gain access to the following collection of interfaces and control panels to manage various aspects of their cloud operations.
Service providers, on Yntraa cloud, gain access to the following collection of interfaces and control panels to manage various aspects of their cloud operations.

Scalable, secure, and flexible cloud storage solutions that support both block and object storage types. Ensure efficient data management with high availability, redundancy, and data protection features.

The Monitoring & Usage section gives you a simple way to track the health and performance of your infrastructure. It shows the status of servers, networks, firewalls, storage, and URLs, along with alerts, uptime, and utilization details.

metadata.title: Welcome
metadata.description: Docusaurus blogging features are powered by the blog plugin.

blogPosts.content:
[Docusaurus blogging features](https://docusaurus.io/docs/blog) are powered by the [blog plugin](https://docusaurus.io/docs/api/plugins/@docusaurus/plugin-content-blog).

Here are a few tips you might find useful.

<!-- truncate -->

Simply add Markdown files (or folders) to the `blog` directory.

Regular blog authors can be added to `authors.yml`.

The blog post date can be extracted from filenames, such as:

- `2019-05-30-welcome.md`
- `2019-05-30-welcome/index.md`

A blog post folder can be convenient to co-locate blog post images:

![Docusaurus Plushie](./docusaurus-plushie-banner.jpeg)

The blog supports tags as well!

**And if you don't want a blog**: just delete this directory, and use `blog: false` in your Docusaurus config.

metadata.title: MDX Blog Post
metadata.description: Blog posts support Docusaurus Markdown features, such as MDX.

blogPosts.content:
Blog posts support [Docusaurus Markdown features](https://docusaurus.io/docs/markdown-features), such as [MDX](https://mdxjs.com/).

:::tip

Use the power of React to create interactive blog posts.

:::

{/* truncate */}

For example, use JSX to create an interactive button:

```js
<button onClick={() => alert('button clicked!')}>Click me!</button>
```

<button onClick={() => alert('button clicked!')}>Click me!</button>

metadata.title: Long Blog Post
metadata.description: This is the summary of a very long blog post,
